rationale.root_cause: They pinpointed the unsafe copy at program.c:12 where strcpy copies argc[1] into buf[256] without bounds and explained the command-line argument can exceed 256 bytes, causing a stack buffer overflow. They explicitly described the overflow of the stack buffer due to the oversized argv data.
rationale.local_fix: They replaced the unbounded copy with a length-checked path that rejects inputs >= sizeof(buf) and then copies strlen+1 bytes, preserving the NUL terminator. This satisfies bounded copy, rejection of overlong input, and ensured null-termination.
rationale.global_fix: They recommended removing unbounded string APIs in favor of size-aware ones, carrying lengths/capacities, and validating argv centrally before copying. This addresses the root cause by eliminating raw, unbounded string handling from the data path.
